title: Kitchen Cabinet with a 2x4 Base
summary: A modular base cabinet built to a repeatable width — plywood sides ripped to an exact, consistent depth, a 2x4/2x6 toekick, and drawers sized to whatever slide hardware you choose.
description:
This is a template for a single base cabinet, sized here as an 18" drawer base but built to be widened or narrowed for any cabinet in a row — **the plywood must be ripped to exactly the same width for every cabinet you build**, since that consistency is what lets a whole run of cabinets line up cleanly at the countertop. Assemble the two sides, bottom, and top/back supports into a square carcass first.

The toekick is built up separately from 2x6 stock and sized to fit the full run of cabinets rather than one box at a time — plan the toekick after all the cabinet boxes in a row are set in place, not before. The plywood back closes the carcass in, and trim between drawer openings finishes the visible face.

**Drawer faces are cut from 1x12 and 1x6 boards, ripped down as needed** — the exact drawer box dimensions depend entirely on which drawer slide hardware is chosen, so treat the drawer cut list as a starting template and adjust to your actual slides' clearance requirements.

materials.name: 3/4" cabinet-grade plywood, ripped to 23-1/4" strips, 4x8 sheet
1x2, matching wood species or paint grade
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: 18" drawer base shown — adjust width to customize.

cutList.material: 3/4" cabinet-grade plywood, ripped to 23-1/4" strips, 4x8 sheet
1x2, matching wood species or paint grade

steps.title: Rip the plywood consistently
steps.body: Rip all plywood for the carcass to the exact same width across every cabinet you're building in this row.
materials: 3/4" cabinet-grade plywood, ripped to 23-1/4" strips, 4x8 sheet

steps.title: Build the carcass
steps.body: Assemble the sides, bottom, and top/back supports into a square box.
materials: 3/4" cabinet-grade plywood, ripped to 23-1/4" strips, 4x8 sheet

steps.title: Add the trim and back
steps.body: Attach the trim between drawer openings, then nail on the plywood back.
materials: 1x2, matching wood species or paint grade

steps.title: Install slides and build drawer faces
steps.body: Mount the drawer slides per their clearance requirements, then rip and cut the drawer faces to fit.

steps.title: Build the toekick
steps.body: Once all cabinet boxes in the row are set in place, build the 2x6 toekick to fit the full run.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly
